Based on the retrieved evidence, I now know that the maxilla is indeed the most commonly affected site among all regions — specifically cited as having a frequency of **62%**, followed by the skull (**15%**) and mandible (**8%**).
This data supports earlier assertions made by agents regarding the preponderance of maxillary occurrence. Furthermore, while the mandibular lesions show higher recurrence rates post-surgery (33%), this does not alter the fact that they occur far less frequently compared to maxillae during initial presentations.
Thus, my agreement remains strong given robust quantitative reporting in literature citing exact regional distribution frequencies.
